volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Rope (Section 5.2.1313 - Section 5.3.1366b)

sections.label: Section 5.2.1313
sections.original: Nummi octingenti aúrei in marsuppio infuerunt ,
sections.translation: Eight hundred golden pieces were there in a purse , besides a hundred Philippean minae in a wash-leather bag apart .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1315
sections.original: Magna hercle praedast , largiter mercedis indipiscar ;
sections.translation: Troth , it is a noble prize ; I shall be getting a handsome reward . The Gods show respect to mortals ; therefore I shall come off bounteously rewarded . No doubt , it is this man ’ s wallet . ( To LABRAX .) Do you proceed to relate the rest .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1318
sections.original: Talentum argenti commodum magnum inerat in crumina ,
sections.translation: A large talent of silver of full weight was in a purse , besides a bowl , a goblet , a beaker , a boat , and a cup .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1320
sections.original: Papae , divitias tu quidém habuisti luculentas .
sections.translation: Astonishing ! you really did have some splendid riches .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1321
sections.original: Miserum istuc verbum et pessimum est , habuisse , et nihil habere .
sections.translation: A shocking expression is that , and a most abominable one . You did have , and now have not .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1322
sections.original: Quid dare velis , qui istaec tibi investiget indicetque ?
sections.translation: What would you be ready to give to one who should find these out for you , and give you information ? Say , speedily and at once .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1323b
sections.translation: Three hundred didrachms .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1323c
sections.translation: Rubbish .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1324
sections.translation: Four hundred .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1324b
sections.translation: Old thrums .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1324c
sections.translation: Five hundred .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1324d
sections.translation: A rotten nut .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1325
sections.translation: Six hundred .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1325b
sections.translation: You are prating about mere tiny weevils .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1326
sections.translation: I ’ ll give seven hundred .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1326b
sections.original: Os calet tibi , nunc id frigefactas .
sections.translation: Your mouth is hot , you are cooling it [*] just now .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1327
sections.translation: I ’ ll give a thousand didrachms .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1327b
sections.translation: You are dreaming .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1327c
sections.translation: I add no more ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.1327d
sections.translation: be off with you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1327e
sections.translation: Hear me then ; if , I ’ faith , I should be off from here , I shan ’ t be here .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1328b
sections.translation: You are asleep .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1329
sections.translation: Say how much you ask .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1329b
sections.translation: That you mayn ’ t be adding anything against your inclination ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1330
sections.original: talentum magnum . non potest triobolum hinc abesse .
sections.translation: a great talent ; it ’ s not possible for three obols to be bated thence ; then do you say either yes or no at once .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1331b
sections.translation: What ’ s to be done here ? It ’ s a matter of necessity , I see : ( to GRIPUS ) the talent shall be paid .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1332b
sections.original: Accede dum huc : Venus haec volo adroget te .
sections.translation: Just step this way ; I wish Venus here to put the question to you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1333
sections.original: Quod tibi libet id mi impera .
sections.translation: Whatever you please , that command me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1333b
sections.translation: Touch this altar of Venus .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1333c
sections.translation: I am touching it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1334
sections.original: Per Venerem hanc iurandum est tibi .
sections.translation: By Venus here must you swear to me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1334b
sections.translation: What must I swear ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1334c
sections.translation: What I shall bid you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1335
sections.original: Praei vérbis quidvis . íd quod domi est , numquam ulli supplicabo .
sections.translation: Dictate in words just as you like . ( Aside .) What I ’ ve got at home , I shall never beg [*] of any one else .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1336
sections.translation: Take hold of this altar .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1336b
sections.translation: I am taking hold of it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1336c
sections.translation: Swear that you will pay me the money on that same day on which you shall gain possession of the wallet .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1337b
sections.translation: Be it so .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1338
sections.original: Venus Cýrenensis , testem te testor mihi ,
sections.translation: Venus of Cyrene , I invoke thee as my witness , if I shall find that wallet which I lost in the ship ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1340
sections.original: cum auro atque argento salvom investigavero
sections.translation: safe with the gold and silver , and it shall come into my possession — Then to this Gripus do I promise ; say so and place your hand upon me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1343
sections.original: Tum ego húic Gripo ( dico , Venus , ut tu audias )
sections.translation: Then to this Gripus do I promise , Venus , do thou hear me —

sections.label: Section 5.2.1344
sections.translation: That I will forthwith give him a great talent of silver . "

sections.label: Section 5.2.1345
sections.original: Si quid fraudassis , dic ut te in quaestu tuo
sections.translation: If you defraud me , say , may Venus utterly destroy your body , and your existence in your calling . ( Aside .) As it is , do you have this for yourself , when you ’ ve once taken the oath .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1348
sections.original: Illaec advorsum si quid peccasso , Venus ,
sections.translation: If , Venus , I shall do anything amiss against this oath , I supplicate thee that all Procurers may henceforth be wretched .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1350
sections.original: Tamen fíet , etsi tu fidem servaveris .
sections.translation: As it is , it shall be so , even if you do keep your oath . Do you wait here ; ( going towards the cottage ) — I ’ ll at once make the old gentleman come out ; do you forthwith demand of him that wallet .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1353
sections.original: Si maxime mi illum reddiderit vidulum ,
sections.translation: If ever so much he shall restore to me this wallet , I ’ m not this day indebted to him three obols even .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1355
sections.original: meus arbitratust , lingua quod iuret mea .
sections.translation: It ’ s according to my own intention what my tongue swears . ( The door opens .) But I ’ ll hold my peace ; see , here he ’ s coming out , and bringing the old man .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1357
sections.translation: Follow this way .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1357b
sections.translation: Where is this Procurer ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1357c
sections.original: Heus tu . ém tibi , hic habet vidulum .
sections.translation: Hark you ( to LABRAX ) , see now ; this person ( pointing at DAEMONES ) has got your wallet .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1358
sections.original: Habeo et fateor esse apud me , et , si tuos est , habeas tibi .
sections.translation: I have got it , and I confess that it is in my possession ; and if it ’ s yours , you may have it . Everything , just as each particular was in it , shall in like manner be given safe to you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1360
sections.translation: ( Holding it out .) Take it , if it ’ s yours .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1360b
sections.original: O di immortales , meus est . salve , vidule .
sections.translation: Immortal Gods , it is mine . ( Takes it .) Welcome , dear wallet .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1361
sections.translation: Is it yours ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1361b
sections.original: Rogitas ? si quidem hercle Iovis fuit , meus est tamen .
sections.translation: Do you ask the question ? If indeed , I ’ faith , it were in Jove ’ s possession , still it is my own .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1362
sections.original: Omnia insunt salva ; una istinc cistella excepta est modo
sections.translation: Everything in it is safe ; there has only been one casket taken out of it , with some trinkets , by means of which this day I have found my daughter .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1364
sections.translation: What person ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1364b
sections.original: Tua quae fuit Palaestra , ea filia inventast mea .
sections.translation: Palaestra , who was your property , she has been discovered to be my own daughter .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1365
sections.original: Bene mehercle factum est . cum istaec res tibi ex sententia
sections.translation: By my troth , it has happily turned out so ; since this matter has happened so fortunately for you according to your wishes , I ’ m rejoiced .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1366b
sections.translation: In that I don ’ t readily believe you .
